PARAM.description: a template for the exception message should the check fail. The message is formed by replacing each {@code %s} placeholder in the template with an argument. These are matched by position - the first {@code %s} gets {@code errorMessageArgs[0]}, etc. Unmatched arguments will be appended to the formatted message in square braces. Unmatched placeholders will be left as-is.
PARAM.name: errorMessageTemplate
PARAM.content: a template for the exception message should the check fail. The message is formed by replacing each {@code %s} placeholder in the template with an argument. These are matched by position - the first {@code %s} gets {@code errorMessageArgs[0]}, etc. Unmatched arguments will be appended to the formatted message in square braces. Unmatched placeholders will be left as-is.

PARAM.description: the arguments to be substituted into the message template. Arguments are converted to strings using {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.
PARAM.name: errorMessageArgs
PARAM.content: the arguments to be substituted into the message template. Arguments are converted to strings using {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.

RETURN.description: reference, guaranteed to be non-null, for convenience
RETURN.content: reference, guaranteed to be non-null, for convenience
